name: Saint Catherine's Monastery
description: The world's oldest active monastery, built by Emperor Justinian in the 6th century. Experience a unique spiritual journey among rare manuscripts and Byzantine icons.

name: Ras Mohammed National Park
description: Egypt's first national park, famous for world-class coral reefs.dating back millions of years. Enjoy swimming in the Magic Lake and exploring rare mangrove forests.

name: Salah El-Din Castle
description: An Islamic fortress built in 1170 AD to protect Egypt's borders and pilgrim routes. Enjoy a short boat trip to the island and a panoramic view overlooking 4 countries.

name: The Blue Hole
description: One of the most famous diving spots in the world, a submarine sinkhole that offers breathtaking coral reefs and crystal-clear waters.A deep natural submarine sinkhole, ranked as one of the most dangerous yet beautiful diving spots worldwide. It offers an adrenaline rush alongside indescribable coral colors.

name: Mount Sinai
description: The historic site where Prophet Moses received the Ten Commandments, a global religious landmark. Experience a thrilling climb to witness the most beautiful sunrise of your life.

name: Moses Springs
description: A historical site featuring natural sulfur springs, believed to be the wells that gushed for Prophet Moses. It serves as a historical and therapeutic tourist attraction.Home to 12 natural springs, linked to the biblical story of the Exodus. The site is perfect for relaxation and learning about Sinai’s religious history amidst palm groves.

name: Colored Canyon
description: A breathtaking natural rock formation created by mineral deposits over thousands of years. Experience a thrilling hike between narrow walls glowing with rainbow colors.

name: Nabq Protected Area
description: A unique protected area blending mountain, desert, and marine environments, featuring the northernmost mangrove forest. Ideal for camping in pristine nature and birdwatching.

name: Tiran Island
description: A strategic island at the Straits of Tiran, a significant spot in modern maritime history. Experience a full-day boat trip with snorkeling stops in the clearest waters imaginable.

name: Blue Lagoon
description: A piece of heaven on Sinai's land, this shallow coastal lagoon features calm turquoise waters with no waves, making it a historic destination for those seeking solitude and primitive camping away from technology. You will experience absolute relaxation under the stars and enjoy kitesurfing in ideal conditions found nowhere else in the world.

name: Abu Galum Reserve
description: A unique natural reserve on the Gulf of Aqaba, blending majestic mountain landscapes with pristine marine environments. It is the ancestral home of Bedouin tribes who have preserved their heritage for millennia. The experience begins with a boat trip or a camel trek through rugged mountain paths to reach shores rich in rare coral reefs and colorful marine life.

name: Three Pools
description: A famous diving and snorkeling spot south of Dahab, consisting of three natural sea pools connected in the heart of the Red Sea. These pools were formed by natural elements to become a global tourist attraction. The site is perfect for families and beginners to enjoy watching colorful fish and corals in shallow waters that are completely safe for underwater photography.

name: Serabit el-Khadim
description: Located atop a rugged mountain, this is the only temple built by Ancient Egyptians (Middle and New Kingdoms) in the heart of Sinai to honor goddess Hathor, "Lady of Turquoise," and protect nearby turquoise mines. You will experience a true adventure climbing the mountain to reach the temple, where you'll be amazed by the stelae and hieroglyphic inscriptions detailing ancient mining expeditions.

name: Shark's Bay
description: A vibrant tourist area in Sharm El Sheikh, historically known as a fishing hub before transforming into one of the city's most upscale bays. Its name reportedly comes from the presence of sharks in its deep waters in the past. The bay offers a complete recreational experience where you can enjoy various water sports or relax in international restaurants overlooking the enchanting Tiran Island.

name: Naama Bay
description: Known as the "beating heart" of Sharm El Sheikh, it was the first tourist area developed in the city and has hosted many important international conferences. You will enjoy walking through the famous promenade amidst lights and music, shopping in bazaars for handmade products, and relaxing on its smooth sandy beaches.

name: Soho Square
description: The most upscale entertainment complex in Sinai, designed after major European squares to become a global attraction in Sharm El Sheikh, featuring artistic statues and stunning walkways. You will experience luxury, including watching "Dancing Fountain" shows, ice skating, or visiting the "Culturama" cinema showcasing Egypt's history.

name: Al Sahaba Mosque
description: A modern architectural icon in the heart of the Old Market, built in Ottoman style with unique artistic touches, making it one of the most beautiful 21st-century mosques in Egypt and the world. The experience is not limited to prayer; you can wander the outer courtyard to take photos of the towering minarets reaching Sharm's sky and visit the Islamic library inside.

name: Hammam Faroun
description: A natural sulfur spring area flowing from a mountain cave with temperatures reaching 70°C, historically renowned as a healing site since ancient times for treating rheumatic diseases. You will experience a unique natural "sauna" inside the cave while inhaling sulfur steam amidst a stunning view of the Gulf of Suez.

name: Wadi Genay
description: A stunning desert oasis tucked away between the majestic granite mountains of Dahab. This valley is a paradise for adventure seekers, offering some of the best rock climbing spots in Sinai and thrilling 4x4 safari tracks. You will experience the true Bedouin life, sitting under palm trees and sipping traditional tea brewed over a wood fire.

name: Castle Zaman
description: A unique tourist landmark built entirely of natural stone in the style of ancient fortresses atop a high plateau, reviving eco-friendly and traditional architecture in Nuweiba. You will experience royal relaxation by swimming in the stone pool and dining on "Slow Food" cooked in underground clay pots for hours.

name: Wishwashi Canyon
description: A hidden lake formed by rainwater between granite mountains in Nuweiba, considered one of the "hidden gems" unknown to many tourists, with water that remains cool year-round. Your experience begins with a thrilling mountain climb ending in a jump into clear freshwater surrounded by towering mountains in a majestic scene.

name: Seven Girls Monastery
description: A small monastery located in the heart of the historical "Wadi Feiran," said to be built in honor of seven nuns who chose isolation and worship, witnessing the beginnings of monasticism in Sinai. You will feel peace and serenity wandering through the monastery garden with its olive and palm trees and simple stone buildings telling stories from early centuries.

name: Sharm El Sheikh Museum
description: The first-ever archaeological museum in Sinai, designed as a world-class cultural hub. It tells the story of the Egyptian civilization with a unique focus on "Humanity and Nature," showcasing rare artifacts, royal chariots, and stunning statues of ancient gods. The museum’s lighting and modern display methods offer a mesmerizing journey through time.

name: Ain Khudra
description: A green oasis in the heart of the arid desert, featuring a natural water well and archaeological inscriptions left by pilgrims and travelers since the Nabataean and Byzantine eras on surrounding rocks. You will live a true safari experience, drinking Bedouin tea with sage and witnessing the stunning contrast between golden sands and lush green palm trees.

name: Farsha Lounge
description: An entertainment spot geniusly built on a cliffside in Sharm El Sheikh, with a decor based on antiques reflecting the region's heritage and culture. You will experience a stunning visual journey at sunset as colorful lanterns and lights blend into an artistic painting against the sea, accompanied by soothing music.

name: Old Market
description: A historical commercial area that was the nucleus of Sharm El Sheikh, evolving into a cultural hub with traditional restaurants and bazaars showcasing Sinai handicrafts and rare spices. You will enjoy a lively shopping experience amidst authentic Egyptian atmosphere, with art shows in the large plaza next to the massive Al-Sahaba Mosque.
